syntax.GenericSyntaxError: A syntax error occurred in your code.
syntax.InvalidAssignmentTargetExpression: Invalid assignment target. You can only assign to variables.
syntax.AssignmentInExpression: You probably meant to use `===`, not `=` here in order to compare two values, not update the variable. Assigning variables inside a statement is not allowed in this editor.
syntax.MissingBacktickToTerminateTemplateLitera: Missing closing backtick `` ` `` to terminate template literal.
syntax.MissingDoubleQuoteToTerminateString:
Did you forget to add end quote? Maybe you meant to write:

```"{{string}}"```
syntax.UnexpectedElseWithoutMatchingIf: There is no `if` statement that this `else` is coming after. Check whether you have too many (or too few) closing braces above this line.
syntax.MissingInitializerInConstDeclaration: Missing initializer in const declaration. Constants must be initialized when declared.
syntax.MissingInitializerInVariableDeclaration: Missing initializer in variable declaration. You must provide a value when declaring a variable with `let`.
syntax.ConstInForLoopInit: You cannot use `const` in a C-style for loop. The loop variable needs to be modified (e.g., i++), but const variables cannot be changed. Use `let` instead, or consider using a for...of loop if you're iterating over an array.
syntax.UnexpectedDoubleIdentifier: Jiki didn't understand what you wanted him to do here.
syntax.MissingDeclarationKeywordWithSuggestion: Did you mean `{{suggestion}}` instead of `{{name}}`? Variable declarations must start with `let` or `const`.
syntax.MissingRightBraceAfterBlock: Missing closing brace `}` after block statement.
syntax.MissingRightBraceInTemplateLiteral: Missing closing brace `}` in template literal interpolation.
syntax.MissingRightBracketInArray: Missing closing bracket `]` to close the list.
syntax.MissingRightBracketInMemberAccess: Missing closing bracket `]` after array index.
syntax.MissingRightParenthesisAfterIfCondition: Missing closing parenthesis `)` after if condition.
syntax.MissingRightParenthesisAfterFunctionCall: Did you forget the closing parenthesis `)` when calling the {{function}} function?
syntax.MissingEndOfLine: Jiki didn't expect `{{current}}` to appear here after the `{{previous}}`.
syntax.MissingVariableName: Missing variable name in declaration. You must provide a name after `let`.
syntax.MultipleStatementsPerLine: Multiple statements found on line {{line}}. Only one statement per line is allowed when oneStatementPerLine is enabled.
syntax.TrailingCommaInArray: Trailing commas are not allowed in lists.
syntax.TrailingCommaInDictionary: Trailing commas are not allowed in objects.
syntax.InvalidDictionaryKey: Invalid object key. Keys must be identifiers, strings, or numbers.
syntax.MissingRightBraceInDictionary: Missing closing brace `}` to close the object.
syntax.UnimplementedToken: The `{{lexeme}}` feature is not available yet at your current level.
syntax.PermanentlyExcludedToken: The `{{lexeme}}` feature is not available in this version of JavaScript.
syntax.NestedFunctionDeclaration: Function declarations are only allowed at the top level, not inside blocks or other functions.
syntax.MissingLeftParenthesisAfterFunctionName: Missing opening parenthesis `(` after function name.
syntax.MissingParameterName: Missing parameter name in function declaration.
syntax.DuplicateParameterName: Duplicate parameter name `{{name}}` in function declaration.
syntax.MissingRightParenthesisAfterParameters: Missing closing parenthesis `)` after function parameters.
syntax.MissingLeftBraceBeforeFunctionBody: Missing opening brace `{` before function body.
syntax.BreakStatementNotAllowed: Jiki doesn't allow you to use `break` in this exercise. Try to solve the exercise just with the ideas we've covered in the lessons.
syntax.ContinueStatementNotAllowed: Jiki doesn't allow you to use `continue` in this exercise. Try to solve the exercise just with the ideas we've covered in the lessons.
syntax.FunctionDeclarationNotAllowed: Jiki doesn't allow you to create your own functions in this exercise. Try to solve the exercise just with the ideas we've covered in the lessons.

syntax.BinaryExpressionNotAllowed: Jiki doesn't allow you to use operators like `+`, `-`, `>`, or `&&` in this exercise. Try to solve the exercise just with the ideas we've covered in the lessons.

lint.ClosingBraceNotOnOwnLine: Put the closing brace `}` on its own line to keep things tidy.
lint.OpeningBraceContentNotOnOwnLine: Put the code inside the braces on a new line to keep things tidy.
lint.IncorrectIndentation:
Jiki expected {{expected}} spaces of indentation here, but you only indented by {{actual}} spaces.

Keeping your code indented correctly will help you avoid a lot of bugs as you can understand how code is nested much more clearly.
lint.IncorrectIndentationAtTopLevel:
Jiki expected {{expected}} spaces of indentation at the start of the line, but there aren't any.

Keeping your code indented correctly will help you avoid a lot of bugs as you can understand how code is nested much more clearly.

runtime.ReturnOutsideFunction: You used the `return` keyword, but you're not inside a function. Return statements can only be used inside functions.
runtime.BreakOutsideLoop: You used the `break` keyword, but you're not inside a loop. Break statements can only be used inside for or while loops.
runtime.ContinueOutsideLoop: You used the `continue` keyword, but you're not inside a loop. Continue statements can only be used inside for or while loops.
runtime.ForOfLoopTargetNotIterable: The for...of loop requires an iterable value (like an array or string), but you provided a {{type}} with value {{value}}.
runtime.MethodNotYetImplemented: The method `{{method}}` exists but has not been implemented yet. It will be available in a future version.
runtime.MethodNotYetAvailable: The method `{{method}}` is not available at your current learning level. Keep progressing to unlock this feature!
runtime.NonJikiObjectDetectedInExecution: Internal error: Expected a JikiObject but received {{receivedType}}: {{receivedValue}}. This is a bug in the interpreter.
runtime.MaxIterationsReached: Your code ran for too long and was stopped to prevent an infinite loop. The maximum number of iterations allowed is {{max}}.
runtime.RepeatCountMustBeNumber: repeat() requires a number, but got {{type}}.
runtime.RepeatCountMustBeNonNegative: repeat() count must be 0 or greater, but got {{value}}.
runtime.RepeatCountTooHigh: repeat() count {{value}} exceeds the maximum of {{max}} iterations.
runtime.FunctionAlreadyDefined: There is already a function called `{{name}}`. Please choose a different name.
runtime.TypeCoercionNotAllowed: Type coercion is not allowed between {{leftType}} and {{rightType}} with operator `{{operator}}`.
runtime.UnaryTypeCoercionNotAllowed: Type coercion is not allowed: the `{{operator}}` operator can't be applied to a {{operand}}.
runtime.StrictEqualityRequired:
In full JavaScript, you can use `==` or `===` to see if two things are equal. However, it's generally considered best practice to use `===` so we have disabled `==` in Jiki.

Please change `==` to `===`.
runtime.ComparisonRequiresNumber: Comparison operator `{{operator}}` requires both values to be numbers.
runtime.NodeNotAllowed: {{nodeType}} is not allowed at your current learning level.
runtime.InvalidNumberOfArguments: This function has {{expected}} input {{slots}} but you provided {{got}} {{inputs}} for Jiki to use with it.
runtime.ForInLoopTargetNotObject: The for...in loop requires an object to iterate over, but you provided a {{type}} with value {{value}}.
runtime.InOperatorRequiresObject: The `in` operator requires an object on the right side, but got {{type}}.
runtime.InOperatorRequiresStringKey: The `in` operator requires a string key on the left side, but got {{type}}.
runtime.InOperatorRequiresIntegerIndex: The `in` operator requires an integer index when used with arrays, but got {{type}}.
runtime.InWithArrayNotAllowed: Although the `in` keyword can be used with arrays, it's a more advanced feature. For now, stick to using it just with dictionaries.
runtime.PropertyNotFoundOnInstance: Property `{{property}}` does not exist on instances of `{{className}}`.
runtime.UnexpectedUncalledFunction: You referenced the `{{name}}` function but didn't actually use it. Did you forget to add `()` at the end?
